name: Chris Griffin
description: American jazz trumpeter of the swing era (1915–2005)
abstract: Gordon Claude "Chris" Griffin (October 31, 1915 – June 18, 2005) was an American jazz trumpeter.

concerts.label: Benny Goodman and His Swing Orchestra

works.t: No, No, Nanette: I Want to Be Happy

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Sauter
about_quotes.evidence: We also played with that band Ben Martin's Riviera at the Jersey shore. In that band was ... Chris Griffin

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer lists Chris Griffin among musicians who 'used to come in all the time and sit in' with the band, but the interviewee (Cozy Cole) does not respond to confirm a personal interaction or relationship.

about_quotes.speaker: Godfrey Hirsch
about_quotes.evidence: I had Chris Griffin, who was with Goodman. And I don't remember the whole gang of it. These guys were fantastic musicians... I enjoyed these things. And I worked with a lot of great musicians.

about_quotes.speaker: Stanley Kay
about_quotes.evidence: We played so many waltzes I forgot how to count four. But, you know, and we laughed. I mean we had good studio musicians — Chris Griffin and Andy Paretti and good players.
about_quotes.doc_title: Stanley Kay interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: It used to be, get after Harry and Chris Griffin. Sometimes they would get drunk, but he wouldn't touch it.

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: That's kind of like the old Goodman brass section, Chris Griffin, Zig Elman and Harry James.
